cd /tmp/sv-747c951ca3264600afbe83f4971d9172/symbols

# Let's try a few .NET 10 versions - download, extract, and check build IDs
for version in 10.0.3 10.0.4 10.0.5 10.0.6 10.0.7; do
  echo "=== Trying version $version ==="
  url="https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64/${version}/microsoft.netcore.app.runtime.android-arm64.${version}.nupkg"
  
  # Download the package
  curl -sSL -o "runtime_${version}.nupkg" "$url" 2>&1
  
  # Extract just libcoreclr.so
  unzip -o -q "runtime_${version}.nupkg" "runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" -d "pkg_${version}" 2>/dev/null
  
  if [ -f "pkg_${version}/runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" ]; then
    # Get build ID
    bid=$(readelf -n "pkg_${version}/runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" 2>/dev/null | grep "Build ID:" | awk '{print $3}')
    echo "Build ID: $bid"
    if [ "$bid" = "4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801" ]; then
      echo "*** MATCH FOUND! Version: $version ***"
      cp "pkg_${version}/runtimes/android-arm64/native/libcoreclr.so" libcoreclr.so
      break
    fi
  else
    echo "libcoreclr.so not found in package"
  fi
  
  # Clean up to save space
  rm -rf "runtime_${version}.nupkg" "pkg_${version}"
done

import subprocess, re

for line in result.stdout.strip().split('\n'):
    parts = line.split(None, 2)
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        addr = int(parts[0], 16)
        stype = parts[1]
        name = parts[2]
        symbols.append((addr, stype, name))
symbols.sort()

# PC offsets from tombstone
offsets = {

    '#02': 0x003a9244,
    '#03': 0x00298b10,
    '#04': 0x00298c48,

}

print(f"NOTE: Using .NET 10.0.3 reference (build ID mismatch - offsets approximate)\n")

for frame, pc in sorted(offsets.items(), key=lambda x: x[0]):
    # Find nearest symbol <= pc
    nearest = None
    for addr, stype, name in symbols:
        if addr <= pc:
            nearest = (addr, name)
        else:
            break
    if nearest:
        offset = pc - nearest[0]
        print(f"  {frame} pc {pc:#010x} => {nearest[1]}+{offset:#x}")
    else:

result = subprocess.run(['readelf', '-sW', 'libcoreclr_ref.so'], capture_output=True, text=True)

for line in result.stdout.strip().split('\n'):
    # Format: Num: Value Size Type Bind Vis Ndx Name
    m = re.match(r'\s*\d+:\s+([0-9a-f]+)\s+(\d+)\s+FUNC\s+\w+\s+\w+\s+\d+\s+(.+)', line)
    if m:
        addr = int(m.group(1), 16)
        size = int(m.group(2))
        name = m.group(3).strip()
        if addr > 0:
            symbols.append((addr, size, name))

symbols.sort()
print(f"Total FUNC symbols: {len(symbols)}")

# Also try nm without demangling to see raw symbols
result2 = subprocess.run(['nm', '--defined-only', 'libcoreclr_ref.so'], capture_output=True, text=True)
raw_symbols = []
for line in result2.stdout.strip().split('\n'):
    parts = line.split(None, 2)
    if len(parts) >= 3:
        addr = int(parts[0], 16)
        raw_symbols.append((addr, parts[2]))
raw_symbols.sort()
print(f"Total nm symbols: {len(raw_symbols)}")

# PC offsets from tombstone
offsets = {
    '#01': 0x003a81f0,
    '#02': 0x003a9244,
    '#03': 0x00298b10,
    '#04': 0x00298c48,
    '#05': 0x0018e3a4,
    '#06': 0x0018df6c,
}

print(f"\nUsing readelf symbols:")
for frame, pc in sorted(offsets.items(), key=lambda x: x[0]):
    nearest = None
    for addr, size, name in symbols:
        if addr <= pc:
            nearest = (addr, size, name)
        else:
            break
    if nearest:
        offset = pc - nearest[0]
        in_func = " (within func)" if offset < nearest[1] else " (past func end)"
        print(f"  {frame} pc {pc:#010x} => {nearest[2]}+{offset:#x}{in_func}")
    else:
        print(f"  {frame} pc {pc:#010x} => (no symbol found)")
